commands.description: List files and directories in the current location.
notes: This intent covers standard non-hidden directory listings.
Placeholder-preserving target commands are ordered first so CLI4ALL keeps path arguments when they are present.

commands.description: List files including hidden entries and detailed metadata.
notes: PowerShell Get-ChildItem -Force is the closest Windows equivalent for showing hidden items.

commands.description: Clear the terminal screen.
notes: This affects terminal display only and does not modify files.

commands.description: Print the current working directory.
notes: Translate Mode keeps its own working directory and reports it explicitly.

commands.description: Change the current working directory.
notes: The desktop Translate Mode intercepts cd and updates its own cwd state before command execution.

commands.description: Print the current user account name.
notes: This is informational and does not modify system state.

commands.description: Show local IP address and network interface configuration.
notes: CLI4ALL prefers ifconfig on macOS and ip addr on Linux for the translated target command.

commands.description: Show DNS resolver configuration and related network details.
notes: The exact DNS detail level differs by platform, so CLI4ALL maps to the closest native inspection command.

commands.description: Ping a host to verify basic network connectivity.
notes: CLI4ALL prefers a bounded four-packet ping on macOS and Linux so Translate Mode does not run indefinitely.

commands.description: Show the currently running processes.
notes: ps aux shows process details for all users.

commands.description: Stop a running process by process identifier.
notes: Killing the wrong process can interrupt services or terminate unsaved work.

commands.description: Create a directory.
notes: Directory creation is safe for automatic Translate Mode execution.

commands.description: Copy a file from one path to another.
notes: Copy operations are treated as low-risk even though an existing destination file may be overwritten.

commands.description: Move or rename a file or directory.
notes: Move and rename operations can replace or relocate important files, so Translate Mode asks for confirmation.

commands.description: Print the contents of a text file.
notes: This intent is intended for small text inspection rather than paging through large files.

commands.description: Create an empty file if it does not already exist.
notes: The Windows examples rely on shell redirection; the translated target stays a simple native file-creation command.

commands.description: Remove a file from the filesystem.
notes: Translate Mode requires confirmation before deleting files.

commands.description: Remove a directory recursively.
notes: Recursive directory removal is high-risk and still yields a destructive block when it targets a root path.

commands.description: Open the current directory in the platform file browser.
notes: This delegates to the platform file browser and may open a GUI application.

commands.description: Open a file path or URL using the native desktop handler.
notes: CLI4ALL preserves a single placeholder token for simple path or URL arguments.

commands.description: Copy a simple value to the system clipboard.
notes: Current placeholder matching preserves only a single non-whitespace token for clipboard text.

commands.description: Print the current clipboard contents.
notes: Clipboard commands depend on platform tooling already being installed.

commands.description: Locate the executable that will run for a given command name.
notes: Use this to confirm which binary is first on PATH.

commands.description: Search for a text pattern inside a file.
notes: CLI4ALL treats basic text search as a read-only inspection command.

commands.description: Find files by name from the current directory tree.
notes: This intent is limited to the reviewed current-directory search form and does not attempt broad path rewrites.

commands.description: Download a URL to stdout or the default client output stream.
notes: Simple URL fetches are low-risk, but piping network output into a shell is governed separately by safety rules.

commands.description: Show network connections, listening ports, and socket usage.
notes: The first target command is chosen to preserve a connection-table style output when possible.

commands.description: Show the current network route table.
notes: Route inspection is read-only but platform output formats differ.

commands.description: Open a text file in a default editor.
notes: Editor launch commands open an interactive editor or GUI and therefore require confirmation in Translate Mode.

commands.description: Show broad operating system and host information.
notes: The information density differs by platform, so CLI4ALL chooses the closest native summary command.

commands.description: Show mounted filesystem capacity and usage.
notes: PowerShell reports filesystem drives rather than the POSIX df table.

commands.description: Print the current date and time.
notes: Windows CMD often splits date and time into two commands, while Unix platforms use a single date invocation.

commands.description: Print environment variables for the current shell session.
notes: This is read-only inspection of the current environment.

commands.description: Search the platform package manager for a package.
notes: Package search is a read-only operation and is safe for automatic execution.

commands.description: Install a software package using the platform package manager.
notes: Package installation changes system state and requires confirmation in Translate Mode.

commands.description: Refresh package metadata or upgrade available packages.
notes: Package manager refresh and upgrade commands can prompt for privileges or change package state.
